observations.statement: The contract defines POST /api/v2/download/awards/ as a route that begins generating a ZIP file of award data.
The contract documents required filters and optional columns, file_format, and limit request fields.
The contract documents response status_url, file_name, file_url, and download_request fields.
The current raw MCP profile exposes top-level fields columns, file_format, filters, and limit.
The current raw profile records earlier observations about default limit, max limit, empty filters, and column echo behavior.
The raw profile does not expose important nested filters as first-class searchable request fields.
RowLimitedAwardDownloadViewSet injects award_levels for elasticsearch_awards and elasticsearch_sub_awards and sets constraint_type to row_count before validation.
AwardDownloadValidator requires filters, sets default award_type_codes, and validates common columns and file_format fields.
Download validation uses FILE_FORMATS for allowed file_format values.
BaseDownloadViewSet creates or reuses a DownloadJob, starts backend processing, and returns a response with status_url, file_name, file_url, and download_request.
The status URL is generated from file_name and points to /api/v2/download/status.

response.bodyShape: object with status_url, file_name, file_url, download_request

observations.statement: A bounded keyword request succeeded and returned a download job descriptor.
The live response injected download_types and default award_type_codes including F001-F010.

bodySample.detail: At least one filter is required.

observations.statement: Empty filters returned HTTP 400 with an explicit validation error.
Unsupported file_format xlsx returned HTTP 400 with valid values csv, tsv, and pstxt.
Award download query paths define award_id_piid as a named export column mapped to piid for contract awards.
Award download query paths define recipient_name as a named export column mapped to latest_transaction_search__recipient_name.
The same award mappings define award_id_fain and award_id_uri as separate identifier columns, so award_id_piid is not the only identifier family available.
spending_by_award documentation shows preview field labels Award ID, Recipient Name, Award Amount, and Awarding Agency in the example award-level field list.

source.title: RT1 reviewer evidence for award download columns

observations.statement: Reviewer evidence said the semantic MCP exposed columns only as array[string] with example values award_id_piid and recipient_name, without a full export-column enumeration.
Reviewer live download initiation echoed requested columns [award_id_piid, recipient_name] in download_request.columns.
Reviewer supplemental ZIP inspection reported CSV headers matching the requested raw column IDs award_id_piid and recipient_name exactly.
spending_by_award preview maps Award ID to display_award_id and returns that display value in the response when requested.
spending_by_award preview maps Recipient Name to recipient_name and Awarding Agency to awarding_toptier_agency_name, with agency metadata added when Awarding Agency is requested.
spending_by_award preview maps Award Amount to total_obligation across contract, IDV, and non-loan assistance award mappings.

source.title: USAspending award download common preview-aligned column mappings

observations.statement: Award download query paths define total_obligated_amount as an export column backed by total_obligation for award-level downloads.
Award download query paths define awarding_agency_name as an export column backed by awarding agency name fields for both FPDS-style and FABS-style award mappings.
Award download query paths continue to define explicit export columns award_id_piid and recipient_name alongside alternate identifier columns such as award_id_fain and award_id_uri.

source.title: RT2 reviewer evidence for preview-to-download continuity
source.locator: Structured reviewer report for v2__download__awards acceptance review (RT1-RT2)

observations.statement: Reviewer evidence showed the preview request used labeled fields Award ID, Recipient Name, Award Amount, and Awarding Agency plus preview sort, order, page, and limit controls on the same filter scope later handed to download.

Reviewer evidence showed the successful download response echoed an effective download_request with filters, columns, limit, and file_format, but did not carry preview sort, order, page, or preview field-label schema forward.

The reviewer concluded that preview-to-download continuity is filter-level only and advised pipelines to compare preview and export through deliberately chosen identifier columns rather than preview row position.
